REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS FRANCIS RYAN AND CHRISTINE RYAN'S MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANTS INTENDED TO FILE A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS UNDER CCP 438, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IT RAISES ISSUES OF FACT THAT CANNOT BE DETERMINED AT THIS STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. TO THE EXTENT THE MOTION IS BASED ON CCP 581, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS THE MOVING PARTY FAILS TO PROVIDE ANY SUPPORT FOR DISMISSAL UNDER CCP 581 IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANT IS MOVING UNDER CCP 425.16, THE MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR AS THIS RELIEF IS NOT LISTED AS GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-11-2018 IN 206, DEFENDANTS FRANCIS RYAN AND CHRISTINE RYAN'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE. MARK CHERNEV, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANTS FRANCIS RYAN AND CHRISTINE RYAN APPEARING AS SELF-REPRESENTED LITIGANTS. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/17/18 IS CONTINUED TO 3/25/19 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS THAT DEFENDANTS ARE PROHIBITED FROM USING THE GRILL ON THE DECK DURING THE CONTINUANCE PERIOD. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Chernov (415-956-8100) for Plaintiff/ opposing party via CourtCall.
Francis Ryan (415-350-4752) for Defendant/Moving party in pro per via CourtCall.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: facts. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs FRANCIS RYAN and CHRISTINE RYAN's Motion To Dismiss Complaint is DENIED. To the extent Defendants intended to file a motion for judgment on the pleadings under CCP 438, the motion is denied as it raises issues of fact that cannot be determined at this stage of the litigation. To the extent the motion is based on CCP 581, the motion is DENIED as the moving party fails to provide any support for dismissal under CCP 581 in the points and authorities. To the extent Defendant is moving under CCP 425.16, the motion is OFF CALENDAR as this relief is not listed as grounds for the motion in the Notice of Motion. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
